extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution. The only transition that sets sent:=true is [t2] (send_attempt), whose guard is `not opted_out`; thus sent can become true only while opted_out is false. The only transition that sets opted_out:=true is [t1] (optout), which simultaneously sets sent:=false, so immediately after [t1] the state has opted_out=true, sent=false (property satisfied). No transition ever resets opted_out back to false, so once opted_out=true it stays true; from then on [t2] is permanently disabled (its guard not opted_out is false), meaning sent can never again be set to true. The remaining transitions reachable in the opted_out=true region are [t3] (sets sent:=false), [t4] (sets sent:=false), and repeated [t1] (sets sent:=false) — all keep sent false. Therefore the bad state (opted_out=true AND sent=true) is unreachable for any event sequence (including all sequences up to 6 events), and the property holds.
